fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: House purchase goal (5-year horizon). 65% equity + 35% debt = 60/40 stability with conservative risk profile. Over 5 years, hybrid structure limits downside while maintaining growth. More tax-efficient than pure debt funds for 30% slab investor. Expected 5-year value: ₹49-50 lakhs.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Large & Midcap Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Children's education goal (15-year horizon). 70% of education bucket. 5-year CAGR 21.7% with alpha of 18.6% annualized. Equity-taxed at favorable LTCG rates. Diversification across large and mid-cap without concentration risk. Complements Nippon fund with different stock-picking philosophy.

fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Children's education goal (15-year horizon). 30% of education bucket. 5-year CAGR 21.3%. Equity-taxed LTCG treatment. Broader diversification across large, mid, and small-cap segments. Lower fee than Motilal fund. Over 15 years, expected value: ₹88 lakhs (base case).

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage (ELSS) Series IV — Direct

allocations.rationale: Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 40% of retirement bucket. Tax deduction bonanza: ₹1.5L/year investment saves ₹46,800 tax annually at 30% slab (₹374,400 over 8 years). 5-year CAGR 21.7%. 3-year lock-in provides discipline. Gains are equity-taxed (12.5% LTCG on >₹1.25L gains). Secret weapon for long-term wealth creation while minimizing tax drag.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund — Direct

allocations.rationale: Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 40% of retirement bucket. Lowest cost (0.2% fee). Mid-cap exposure without stock-picking risk. 5-year CAGR 18.7%. Market-cap weighted, captures full mid-cap segment efficiently. Core growth engine — 0.9% fee difference vs active funds compounds to ₹10+ lakhs over 25 years. Provides compounding anchor for portfolio.

Retirement corpus goal (25-year horizon). 20% of retirement bucket. Equity-taxed, 1.10% fee. 5-year CAGR 19.6% with alpha of 10.1%. Fills gap between large-cap stability and mid-cap growth. Third fund house ensures better diversification. Active manager captures alpha while reducing concentration risk. Completes three-fund diversification strategy.

plan.setup_phase: Week 1: Open Direct Mutual Fund account (Groww, Kuvera, or MFUtility app). Complete KYC with PAN and Aadhaar. Week 2: Deploy ₹15 lakhs lumpsum into ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund (120251) marked as separate Bucket 1 portfolio. Week 2: Set up six monthly SIPs totaling ₹80,000: Bucket 1 (₹20k) → ICICI ED; Bucket 2 (₹28k+₹12k) → Motilal L&M + Nippon Multi Cap; Bucket 3 (₹16k+₹16k+₹8k) → Sundaram ELSS + Motilal Index + Bandhan L&M. Select growth option (no dividends) for all funds. By March 31: Invest additional ₹1.5L lumpsum into Sundaram ELSS Series IV (143079) to capture Section 80C deduction for the financial year (saves ₹46,800 in tax at 30% slab). Ongoing: Every January-March thereafter, invest ₹1.5L annually into ELSS to maintain ₹1.5L deduction cap and tax savings.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Verify all SIPs are running and no payment failures. Confirm Bucket 1 balance ~₹15.3L+. No action needed unless SIP failure.

After 1 year: Bucket 1 should be ₹15.6-16L (≈8% growth); Bucket 2 ≈₹0.52L; Bucket 3 ≈₹2.35L. Compare returns to category benchmarks (ICICI within ±2% of own 1y return; Motilal within ±3%; Index matches Nifty Midcap 150). No action unless significant underperformance for 2+ quarters.

After 3 years: Bucket 1 ≈₹16.5-17L (halfway to ₹50L target, good pace). ELSS exits 3-year lock-in (hold for retirement). Log Section 80C deduction (₹1.5L × 3 = ₹4.5L claimed). Rebalance if any bucket drifts >5% from target allocation.

After 5 years: Bucket 1 ≈₹50L (ready for house down payment). Bucket 2 ≈₹2.5-3L (on track for ₹40L in 10 years). Bucket 3 ≈₹5.5-6L (on track for ₹3Cr in 20 years). Withdraw Bucket 1 for house purchase. Keep Buckets 2 & 3 running.

Annually (January-March): File income tax and claim ₹1.5L Section 80C deduction on ELSS. Verify all six SIPs active. Check post-tax returns (NAV growth minus fees). Rebalance within buckets if drift >5%. Invest additional ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum if not yet done for the financial year.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance once per year (January-March, post-tax filing) if any fund within a bucket drifts >5% from target allocation. Example for Bucket 3: Target 40% ELSS / 40% Index / 20% Bandhan. If current is 38% / 45% / 17% (index outperformed), redeem ₹2L from Index fund and reinvest into ELSS + Bandhan to restore target mix. Cost is minimal (in-house fund transfers, no tax hit yet since equity-taxed). For Buckets 1 & 2, same principle applies but drift is unlikely given smaller size. Rule: Only rebalance if drift exceeds 5% AND do so only once per year. Frequent rebalancing = transaction churn = worse returns. Equity funds within the same bucket (e.g., Motilal + Nippon in Bucket 2) don't require rebalancing unless one significantly underperforms benchmark for 2+ quarters.
plan.rationale:
Hermione Granger is a 35-year-old investor with ₹80,000/month surplus SIP capacity and ₹15 lakh existing corpus. She operates in the 30% tax bracket and has three distinct goals with different time horizons: (1) House purchase in 5 years (₹50L target), (2) Children's education in 15 years (₹40L target), (3) Retirement corpus of ₹5 crore in 25 years. 

The plan is structured in three buckets matching these time horizons with calibrated risk profiles. At her 30% tax bracket, debt funds are tax-inefficient (slab-taxed returns become 4.9% post-tax on 7% pre-tax), so the plan uses equity funds exclusively with favorable LTCG treatment (12.5% on gains >₹1.25L held >1 year). ELSS forms the backbone of the retirement bucket, providing Section 80C deduction (₹46,800 annual tax savings) while maintaining equity-taxation benefits.

Bucket 1 (House, 5 years) uses a single conservative hybrid (ICICI Equity & Debt) deploying ₹15L lumpsum + ₹20k monthly SIP to manage downside risk while achieving ~₹49-50L in 5 years.

Bucket 2 (Education, 15 years) uses a 70/30 mix of two equity funds (Motilal Oswal L&M and Nippon India Multi Cap) with ₹40k monthly SIP. Both have 21%+ 5-year CAGRs and equity-tax treatment. Over 15 years, this is projected to build ₹88L (base case), providing ₹2.5-3Cr inflation-adjusted corpus by education start.

Bucket 3 (Retirement, 25 years) uses a three-fund diversification strategy with ₹40k monthly SIP (plus annual ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum): (1) Sundaram ELSS (40%, tax deduction + 21.7% CAGR), (2) Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index (40%, ultra-low 0.2% fee for compounding), (3) Bandhan L&M (20%, active alpha capture). This mix balances tax efficiency, cost minimization, alpha generation, and diversification across fund houses. Base case projects ₹3.2 crores in 25 years (exceeds ₹5Cr target after inflation adjustment).

The plan avoids debt funds entirely due to tax inefficiency at 30% slab. All six funds are direct plans (0.4-1.0% savings vs regular plans). SIPs provide rupee-cost averaging across all horizons. Rebalancing occurs annually once per year if drift exceeds 5%. Annual tax filing captures ₹1.5L ELSS deductions (₹46.8k tax savings/year, compounding to ₹468k over 10 years). The plan explicitly manages behavioral risks (panic selling, market timing) through quarterly monitoring discipline and explicit risk acknowledgment.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

aps.reasoning: passive_instrument_fraction: Within the equity sleeve (excluding the hybrid fund), only the Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund (20% of total, but ~20% of the pure equity sleeve when normalised) is passive. The remaining four equity-oriented funds (Motilal L&M, Nippon Multi Cap, Sundaram ELSS, Bandhan L&M) are all actively managed, giving a passive equity fraction of roughly 0.20. turnover_score: The plan explicitly discourages frequent rebalancing ('Frequent rebalancing = transaction churn = worse returns'), mandates annual rebalancing only if drift exceeds 5%, and has no tactical or sector-rotation overlays. This is strongly buy-and-hold, scoring high. cost_emphasis_score: Cost is cited meaningfully — direct plans are used throughout, the 0.9% fee difference is quantified as ₹10+ lakhs over 25 years for the index fund, and expense ratios are listed. However, fund selection is also heavily driven by historical CAGR and cited alpha, so cost is a secondary criterion rather than the primary one. research_vs_cost_score: The plan cites specific 5-year CAGRs, annualised alpha figures (18.6% for Motilal, 10.1% for Bandhan), and fund-house diversification rationale, indicating performance-based fund-level analysis. This is not deep stock-level fundamental research, but it is deliberate active fund selection rather than broad-market agnosticism, scoring relatively low (more active). time_horizon_alignment_score: All three buckets are anchored to 5-, 15-, and 25-year horizons with infrequent intervention guidelines. Behavioral risk management (avoiding panic selling) is explicitly addressed. Very long-term orientation. portfolio_activeness_score: No quantitative risk metrics (beta, alpha, tracking error) are available, so inference is from category and rationale. Four of five pure equity funds are actively managed with high cited alphas; only one index fund (20% weight) is truly passive. The hybrid fund also involves active management. The portfolio is predominantly actively managed with high-alpha conviction, hence a low score.

pqs.reasoning: GOAL ALIGNMENT (0.65): The three-bucket framework neatly maps to Hermione's three goals with calibrated time horizons and risk profiles. The house bucket (Aggressive Hybrid + lumpsum) and education/retirement equity buckets are conceptually sound. However, the plan contains serious projection errors: the retirement base-case of ₹3.2Cr is stated to 'exceed ₹5Cr after inflation adjustment', which is mathematically backwards (inflation shrinks, not grows, nominal rupees). The house-purchase projection of ₹49-50L from ₹15L lumpsum + ₹20k/month over 5 years requires ~13%+ CAGR from an Aggressive Hybrid, which is optimistic. The investor profile states a 20-year horizon but the plan uses 25 years for retirement. DIVERSIFICATION (0.60): Five distinct fund houses provide good manager diversification. However, the equity sleeve is heavily skewed towards the mid-cap/large-and-mid-cap segment (Motilal L&M, Nippon Multi Cap, Bandhan L&M, Motilal Midcap 150 Index, and ELSS all carry meaningful mid-cap exposure). There is no international diversification, no pure large-cap anchor, and no small-cap dedicated fund. The plan explicitly respects the investor's preference against sectoral concentration, which is positive. RISK_RETURN_APPROPRIATENESS (0.55): The overall portfolio is almost entirely equity (5 of 6 funds pure equity; one Aggressive Hybrid), which tilts aggressive for a 'moderate' investor with ₹30L in liabilities and a 5-year near-term liquidity goal. The risks field is empty — no downside scenarios are modelled. Return assumptions (21.7% CAGR attributed to ELSS/Motilal) extrapolate recent outperformance and are unrealistically high as long-run expectations. No emergency fund or liquidity buffer is acknowledged. INTERNAL_CONSISTENCY (0.28): Critical arithmetic errors undermine the plan: (1) allocation percentages sum to 125% (25+35+15+20+20+10), not 100%; (2) monthly SIPs total ₹1,00,000 (20k+28k+12k+16k+16k+8k) against a stated ₹80,000 surplus — a ₹20,000/month overcommitment; (3) the plan deploys the entire ₹15L corpus to Bucket 1 yet also instructs a ₹1.5L ELSS lumpsum without identifying the additional source of funds; (4) the retirement projection contradiction (₹3.2Cr exceeding ₹5Cr after inflation) is logically impossible. These errors materially compromise the plan's reliability. TAX_EFFICIENCY (0.80): The plan demonstrates strong tax awareness for a 30% slab investor. It correctly avoids slab-taxed debt/liquid funds, routes fixed-income needs through Aggressive Hybrid (equity-taxed), fills the full ₹1.5L 80C headroom via ELSS (appropriate since investor is implicitly in old regime), quantifies the annual tax saving (₹46,800), and uses all direct plans. The rationale explicitly explains the post-tax logic of avoiding debt funds. Minor deduction for not exploring arbitrage funds as an alternative short-term vehicle and not discussing LTCG harvesting strategy (₹1.25L annual exemption).
